page: Microsoft Azure L2as_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Lasv4 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 480 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Virtualization is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is also strong at 13.58 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is limited due to the 2-vCPU configuration, with stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth write speeds are average at 27.68 GB/sec, while read-only memory bandwidth is weak. Database operations under Passmark scored in the bottom 10%, and Redis SET operations reached 3.18 million operations per second, placing in the bottom 25%.

The L2as_v4 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive multi-core parallelization. While single-threaded execution is highly efficient, the instance exhibits performance trade-offs in multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, and database operations. It is best suited for lightweight memory-optimized workloads, single-threaded data processing, and basic caching applications.
description: Microsoft Azure L2as_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Lasv4 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core, 2 threads) clocked at 3.7 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. The instance exhibits strong single-core performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core benchmarks ranking in the top 25%. However, its multi-core and memory read bandwidth benchmarks fall into the bottom 25%, and database operations score in the bottom 10%. With a high 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio, the L2as_v4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, single-threaded applications but presents clear performance trade-offs for highly parallelized or database-intensive workloads. It is best suited for lightweight memory-optimized tasks, single-threaded data processing, and basic caching.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L2as_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC processor, 2 vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local storage, optimized for single-threaded memory-heavy tasks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L2as_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring strong single-core performance and bundled local storage for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
16.0 GB RAM providing a high 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio
480 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Suited for lightweight memory-optimized tasks and single-threaded processing
